title: Enable DDE Support
text: Enable DDE Support Uniface DDE support requires the udde.dll to be loaded when Uniface starts. To enable DDE support: Set the DDE setting in the [udde] section of your .ini file to On . [udde] DDE=on Copy Edit the application's assignment file to add the appropriate module to the [USER_3GL] section. Use the /preload setting. For example: [USER_3GL] usyscom:/bin/udde.dll /preload Copy Create a signature for the UDDE component. For more information, see Define a Component Signature Choose More Editors > Signature . Set the Implementation to C and define the operations with no parameters. Edit the details of each operation to remove the ComponentName prefix that Uniface automatically adds to the Literal Name of C implementations. Save and compile the signature. Once UDDE is enabled, you can use Uniface as a UDDE client or server. Related Links Uniface as a DDE Client Uniface as a DDE Server When Uniface acts as a DDE server, the DDE link must be defined in the client application. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
